targets.candidate_formalization: forall x : Circle.S0.Sign, Circle.S0.antipode x != x
targets.guardrail: Do not identify S0 with C_1.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S0-T0003 and Lean-proved as Circle.S0.antipode_ne.
targets.target_statement: For every S0 point x, antipode x is distinct from x.
targets.title: S0 antipode has no fixed point

targets.candidate_formalization: Circle.S0.antipode Circle.S0.Sign.neg = Circle.S0.Sign.pos and Circle.S0.antipode Circle.S0.Sign.pos = Circle.S0.Sign.neg
targets.guardrail: Keep this as finite opposition, not a cyclic rotation claim.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S0-T0004 and Lean-proved as Circle.S0.antipode_swaps.
targets.target_statement: The S0 antipode sends each named sign to the other sign.
targets.title: S0 antipode swaps the two named signs

targets.candidate_formalization: Function.Bijective Circle.S0.antipode
targets.guardrail: This is a finite two-point bijection, not a general theorem about all antipodal maps.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S0-T0005 and Lean-proved as Circle.S0.antipode_bijective.
targets.target_statement: The two-point antipode is a bijection of the S0 opposition model.
targets.title: S0 antipode is bijective

targets.candidate_formalization: n != 0 -> (Circle.period n k = n iff Nat.gcd n k = 1)
targets.guardrail: Do not restrict the result to prime n; prime full-coil is only the common corollary.
targets.next_action: Promoted to CC-T0054 and Lean-proved as Circle.fullCoil_iff_coprime.
targets.target_statement: On a positive finite circle, stride k is a full coil exactly when gcd(n,k)=1.
targets.title: Full coil iff coprime

targets.candidate_formalization: n != 0 -> sameOrbit n k x y iff Nat.gcd n k divides representative_difference x y
targets.guardrail: Avoid ad hoc integer representatives that are not tied to ZMod equality.
targets.next_action: Promoted to CC-T0061, with CC-T0055, CC-T0059, and CC-T0060 as the quotient, forward, and reverse support theorems.
targets.target_statement: Two nodes lie in the same stride-k orbit exactly when their representatives differ by a multiple of gcd(n,k).
targets.title: Orbit membership by gcd congruence

targets.candidate_formalization: Nat.Coprime n a -> Function.Bijective (fun x => Circle.rot n b (Circle.scale n a x)) plus a composition normal form
targets.guardrail: Do not claim all endomorphisms are affine unless the classification is separately proved.
targets.next_action: Promoted to CC-T0058 with supporting Lean theorems CC-T0056 and CC-T0057; leave full affine classification for a later target.
targets.target_statement: Every map built as a rotation after coprime scaling is a finite-circle bijection, and composition follows affine modular arithmetic.
targets.title: Affine circle automorphism normal form

targets.candidate_formalization: forall n : Nat, forall k : Int, Function.Bijective (Circle.S1.signedRot n k)
targets.guardrail: Keep this finite modular bijection separate from continuous orientation claims.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S1O-T0004 and Lean-proved as Circle.S1.signedRot_bijective.
targets.target_statement: Every signed rotation on a finite circle is a reversible bijection of nodes.
targets.title: Signed rotations are bijections

targets.candidate_formalization: suspendedCircleAntipode n (suspendedCircleLongitudeRotation n stride p) = suspendedCircleLongitudeRotation n (-stride) (suspendedCircleAntipode n p)
targets.guardrail: Do not assign independent longitude coordinates to poles.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S2-T0016 using the signed S1 rotation API on finite suspended-circle equator coordinates.
targets.target_statement: On equator coordinates, rotating longitude and then taking the antipode agrees with taking the antipode and rotating by the opposite stride.
targets.title: Longitude rotation commutes with suspended-circle antipode

targets.candidate_formalization: forall n : Nat, Function.Bijective (Circle.S2.suspendedCircleAntipode n)
targets.guardrail: This is a finite suspended-circle bijection, not a full continuous antipodal-map theorem.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S2-T0018 and Lean-proved as Circle.S2.suspendedCircleAntipode_bijective.
targets.target_statement: The finite suspended-circle antipode is a reversible bijection of suspended-circle points.
targets.title: Suspended-circle antipode is bijective

targets.candidate_formalization: forall n : Nat, forall stride : Int, Function.Bijective (Circle.S2.suspendedCircleLongitudeRotation n stride)
targets.guardrail: This is a finite suspended-circle coordinate action, not the continuous rotation group of S^2.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S2-T0019 and Lean-proved as Circle.S2.suspendedCircleLongitudeRotation_bijective using the signed S1 rotation bijection on equator coordinates.
targets.target_statement: Every signed longitude rotation of the finite suspended-circle model is a reversible bijection of suspended-circle points.
targets.title: Suspended longitude rotations are bijections

targets.candidate_formalization: forall n r stride : Nat, Function.Bijective (Circle.S2.longitudeRotation n r stride)
targets.guardrail: This is a finite sphere-grid coordinate bijection, not a smooth SO(3) action on S^2.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S2-T0020 and Lean-proved as Circle.S2.longitudeRotation_bijective using the finite-circle rotation bijection on each latitude ring.
targets.target_statement: Every finite sphere-grid longitude rotation fixes poles, rotates each latitude ring, and is a reversible bijection of grid points.
targets.title: Sphere-grid longitude rotations are bijections

targets.candidate_formalization: For each latitude ring, orbitClassCount n k = Nat.gcd n k
targets.guardrail: Do not turn the whole sphere grid into a product of two circles.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S2-T0017 as a latitude-ring wrapper around the S1 orbit-class count theorem.
targets.target_statement: Each non-pole latitude ring decomposes under a stride into gcd(n,k) finite cycles.
targets.title: Latitude-ring orbit decomposition inherits S1 orbit count

targets.candidate_formalization: one_mul, mul_one, and inverse equations for Circle.S3.UnitQuaternion
targets.guardrail: Keep noncommutativity explicit.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3Q-T0006 and S3Q-T0007 with bundled identity and conjugate-inverse laws for UnitQuaternion.
targets.target_statement: Unit quaternions have an identity element and conjugate inverse under multiplication.
targets.title: Unit quaternion identity and inverse laws

targets.candidate_formalization: Package S3H-T0004 and S3H-T0005 into a right-action interface for normalized Hopf pairs.
targets.guardrail: Do not claim S3 is globally S2 x S1.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3H-T0006 using HopfPhase, hopfPhaseAct, hopfPhaseIdentity, and hopfPhaseMul wrappers.
targets.target_statement: Identity phase fixes Hopf pairs and phase composition agrees with complex multiplication.
targets.title: Hopf phase action is a right action

targets.candidate_formalization: Setoid UnitQuaternion generated by q and -q
targets.guardrail: Do not claim a complete SO(3) formalization without quotient topology and rotation action proofs.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3S-T0004 by defining spinSignRelated on UnitQuaternion and proving Equivalence.
targets.target_statement: The relation q ~ -q is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive on unit quaternions.
targets.title: Spin sign quotient equivalence relation

targets.candidate_formalization: forall q : Circle.S3.UnitQuaternion, Circle.S3.unitQuaternionConj (Circle.S3.unitQuaternionConj q) = q
targets.guardrail: This is a bundled algebraic unit-quaternion fact, not a complete rotation-space theorem.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3Q-T0008 and Lean-proved as Circle.S3.unitQuaternion_conj_involutive.
targets.target_statement: The bundled conjugate of a unit quaternion returns the original unit quaternion when applied twice.
targets.title: Unit-quaternion conjugation is involutive

targets.candidate_formalization: forall phase : Circle.S3.HopfPhase, Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul Circle.S3.hopfPhaseIdentity phase = phase and Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul phase Circle.S3.hopfPhaseIdentity = phase
targets.guardrail: This is a coordinate phase-algebra law, not a global-product claim about S^3.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3H-T0007 and Lean-proved as Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul_identity.
targets.target_statement: The Hopf phase identity is a left and right identity for the coordinate phase multiplication wrapper.
targets.title: Hopf phase multiplication has identity

targets.candidate_formalization: forall left middle right : Circle.S3.HopfPhase, Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul (Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul left middle) right = Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul left (Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul middle right)
targets.guardrail: This remains an algebraic coordinate wrapper law and does not formalize the full Hopf fibration topology.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S3H-T0008 and Lean-proved as Circle.S3.hopfPhaseMul_assoc.
targets.target_statement: Composing Hopf phase multiplications is associative in the coordinate phase wrapper.
targets.title: Hopf phase multiplication is associative

targets.candidate_formalization: eulerCharacteristic (iteratedSuspensionCounts m counts) = alternatingSuspensionEuler m (eulerCharacteristic counts), with a two-step parity corollary.
targets.guardrail: Keep this finite-cell-count only, not a smooth classification theorem.
targets.next_action: Promoted to COMMON-T0004 and COMMON-T0005 as the general recursive iteration theorem and two-step parity corollary.
targets.target_statement: Iterated finite suspensions alternate Euler characteristic according to suspension parity.
targets.title: General iterated suspension Euler parity

targets.candidate_formalization: A manifest/paper theorem boundary rather than a Lean mathematical theorem.
targets.guardrail: Do not imply the project settles the S6 complex-structure problem.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S6-T0003 as a Lean warning-boundary marker tied to S6-W0001; no complex-structure theorem is claimed.
targets.target_statement: The current S6 finite model proves only cell-count and Euler facts, not complex-structure claims.
targets.title: S6 warning theorem boundary

targets.candidate_formalization: forall counts : List Nat, Circle.Common.eulerCharacteristic (Circle.Common.suspensionCounts (Circle.Common.suspensionCounts counts)) = Circle.Common.eulerCharacteristic counts
targets.guardrail: This is finite cell-count Euler algebra, not stable homotopy or smooth suspension equivalence.
targets.next_action: Promoted to COMMON-T0006 and Lean-proved as Circle.Common.suspensionEuler_two_step.
targets.target_statement: Applying the finite suspension cell-count transform twice preserves Euler characteristic.
targets.title: Direct double suspension preserves Euler characteristic

targets.candidate_formalization: quaternionicPhaseRotate (p * q) = quaternionicPhaseRotate q after quaternionicPhaseRotate p, plus base invariance
targets.guardrail: Do not claim full fibration topology.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S7QH-T0003 with coordinate right-phase composition and base-invariance laws.
targets.target_statement: Right multiplication by unit quaternion phases composes according to quaternion multiplication and preserves the quaternionic Hopf base point.
targets.title: Quaternionic Hopf right phase action composition

targets.candidate_formalization: forall o, normSq o = 1 -> (conj o) * o = 1 and o * (conj o) = 1
targets.guardrail: Do not introduce a group API for unit octonions.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S7O-T0008 with bracket-explicit left and right conjugate inverse equations for norm-one octonions.
targets.target_statement: For a norm-one octonion, conjugation gives left and right inverse equations with explicit bracketing.
targets.title: Unit octonion inverse equations with bracketing

targets.candidate_formalization: forall p u, Circle.S7.quaternionPairNormSq (Circle.S7.quaternionPairRightPhase p u) = Circle.S7.quaternionPairNormSq p * Circle.S7.quaternionCoordNormSq u
targets.guardrail: This is a bounded coordinate norm law, not a complete quaternionic Hopf fibration theorem.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S7QH-T0004 and Lean-proved as Circle.S7.quaternionPairRightPhase_norm_scaled.
targets.target_statement: Right multiplication of both quaternion coordinates by a phase scales the total pair norm by the phase norm.
targets.title: Quaternionic right phase scales pair norm

targets.candidate_formalization: forall p u, Circle.S7.quaternionCoordNormSq u = 1 -> Circle.S7.quaternionPairNormSq (Circle.S7.quaternionPairRightPhase p u) = Circle.S7.quaternionPairNormSq p
targets.guardrail: This preserves normalized coordinate pairs under unit phase; it is not quotient topology.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S7QH-T0005 and Lean-proved as Circle.S7.quaternionPairRightPhase_unit_preserves_norm.
targets.target_statement: Shared right multiplication by a unit quaternion preserves the total norm of a quaternion pair.
targets.title: Unit quaternionic right phase preserves pair norm

targets.blocker: S15-T0008 proves a unit-basis counterexample: naive shared right unit-octonion multiplication does not preserve the full bounded Hopf coordinate.
targets.candidate_formalization: octonionicHopfMap (x*u, y*u) = octonionicHopfMap (x,y) under norm-one and bracketed assumptions
targets.guardrail: Do not claim full octonionic Hopf fibration or quotient topology.
targets.next_action: Use S15-T0005 through S15-T0007 as the safe right-phase landing facts; future full invariance needs a different bracketed quotient/fiber model.
targets.target_statement: Shared right unit-octonion phase should preserve the bounded octonionic Hopf landing coordinates where the nonassociative formula is explicitly bracketed.
targets.title: Octonionic Hopf phase-invariance target

targets.candidate_formalization: forall cells : List Nat, Circle.Common.eulerCharacteristic (Circle.Future.S15.eightSuspensions cells) = Circle.Common.eulerCharacteristic cells
targets.guardrail: This is finite suspension/Euler parity only, not the full octonionic Hopf fibration.
targets.next_action: Promoted to S15-T0009 and Lean-proved as Circle.Future.S15.eightSuspensions_eulerCharacteristic.
targets.target_statement: Eight finite suspension steps preserve Euler characteristic, giving the finite S15 topological anchor a direct parity theorem.
targets.title: Eight suspensions preserve Euler characteristic

targets.candidate_formalization: Validity predicate over generated theorem metadata and proof glyph fields.
targets.guardrail: A glyph is a proof interface, not a proof substitute.
targets.next_action: Promoted to P2G-T0004 with P2G-T0005 as the matching-metadata constructor theorem; generated JSON integration remains a site/exporter layer.
targets.target_statement: A proof-carrying glyph certificate is valid only if its theorem id resolves to a manifest entry and its Lean name matches generated theorem data.
targets.title: Glyph certificate resolves theorem manifest entry

targets.candidate_formalization: Transition maps over a finite base with cocycle-like compatibility on triple overlaps.
targets.guardrail: Do not treat twisted bundles as global products.
targets.next_action: Promoted to P2BU-T0007 with base-preserving projection and fiber-map support theorems P2BU-T0005 and P2BU-T0006; true overlap/cocycle data remains future work.
targets.target_statement: Future nontrivial bundle transition functions must preserve base coordinates and compose on overlaps.
targets.title: Bundle transition sanity target

targets.candidate_formalization: Executable benchmark protocol plus bounded phase-coordinate theorem links.
targets.guardrail: Benchmark results are not Lean proofs.
targets.next_action: Deterministic known-period fixture added; expand to noisier synthetic cases and periodogram/Fourier baselines before usefulness claims.
targets.target_statement: Coil signatures should be compared against standard periodogram/autocorrelation baselines on synthetic periodic data before usefulness claims.
targets.title: Coil signatures versus ordinary period baselines

targets.candidate_formalization: MLX benchmark harness plus finite index theorems already present in AI manifests.
targets.guardrail: No claim that circles improve all AI.
targets.next_action: Tiny deterministic phase-channel, backend-parity, learned-feature, harmonic/Fourier-feature, cyclic-memory, coil-retrieval, content-gated retrieval, learned content-gate retrieval, looped-recurrence, token-level recurrence routing, learned token-level recurrence routing, training-free loop-wrapper, middle-block recurrence, learned middle-block recurrence, multi-resolution recurrence, learned recurrence-schedule, adapter-block, adapter parameter-budget, circulant-mixer, MultiCoil/RoPE-style, and RoPE-style relative phase fixtures added; expand toward learned dense-model, standard RoPE, LoRA, attention, Universal Transformer, fixed/adaptive looped transformer, learned multi-resolution recurrence, recurrent-memory, sparse/MoE, state-space, and actual MLX model baselines before quality claims.
targets.target_statement: Phase-channel, cyclic-memory, adapter-block, circulant-mixer, MultiCoil/RoPE-style, and looped/recursive transformer AI ideas should be benchmarked on Mac/MLX against standard baselines before making model-quality claims.

targets.candidate_formalization: Extend benchmark_coil_layout.py with reproducible fixtures and record results without overclaiming.
targets.guardrail: Performance claims require benchmarks.
targets.next_action: Benchmark parameter grid and expected-output checks added; expand to workload-specific direct, dense, FFT, NTT, and MLX layout baselines before performance claims.
targets.target_statement: Circular/stride layout claims should be benchmarked against dense, direct, FFT, and layout baselines.
targets.title: Coil layout memory-locality benchmark
